checkYourRequest.description: Title of the page where the user can check their request before publishing it.

yourDetails.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your request” page that shows the user’s details.

publishedName.description: Label for the name on the “Check your request” page that will be published alongside the request.

changeName.description: Link that allows the user to change their name on the “Check your request” page.

nowPublishYourRequest.description: Heading for the section on the “Check your request” page that allows the user to publish their request.

weWillShareYourRequest.message: We’ll share your request with the PREreview community.
weWillShareYourRequest.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Now publish your request” that explains what will happen when the user publishes their request.

requestPrereview.description: Label for the button that publishes the user’s request for a PREreview.

requestAPrereview.description: Title of the page that allows the user to request a PREreview of a preprint.

sorryWeAreHavingProblems.description: Heading of the page that is shown when there is a problem publishing the user’s request for a PREreview.

unableToPublishYourWorkWasSaved.message: We were unable to publish your request for a PREreview. We saved your work.
unableToPublishYourWorkWasSaved.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems” that explains that the user’s request for a PREreview could not be published, but their work was saved.

pleaseTryAgainLater.message: Please try again later by coming back to this page.
pleaseTryAgainLater.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems” that advises the user to try again later.

ifProblemPersistsContactUs.message: If this problem persists, please <mailtoHelp>get in touch</mailtoHelp>.
ifProblemPersistsContactUs.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Sorry, we’re having problems” that advises the user to contact support if the problem persists.

contactDetails.description: Title of the page that asks you to provide your email address.

confirmEmailAddressFirst.message: We’re ready to publish your request, but we need to confirm your email address first.
confirmEmailAddressFirst.description: Text shown alongside the title “Contact details”.

onlyUseToContactYou.message: We’ll only use this to contact you about your account and what you publish.
onlyUseToContactYou.description: Text shown alongside the title “Contact details” that explains how we’ll use your email address.

whatIsYourEmailAddress.description: Label for the text field on the “Contact details” form for the user’s email address.

whatIsYourEmailAddressErrorMissing.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the “Contact details” form without answering the question “What is your email address?”.

whatIsYourEmailAddressErrorInvalid.message: Enter an email address in the correct format, like {exampleEmailAddress}
whatIsYourEmailAddressErrorInvalid.description: Error message shown when the user tries to complete the “Contact details” form and answers the question “What is your email address?” with an invalid email address.

verifyYourEmailAddress.description: Title of the page that says you need follow the link in the email we sent to verify the email address.

followLinkInEmail.message: Please open the email we sent to {emailAddress} and follow the link. You’ll then be able to publish your request.
followLinkInEmail.description: Text shown alongside the title “Verify your email address” that explains what the user needs to do to verify their email address.

closePageOnceVerified.message: Once your address is verified, you can close this page.
closePageOnceVerified.description: Text shown alongside the title “Verify your email address” that explains what to do after verifying your email address.

beNotifiedWhenReviewPublished.message: Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?
beNotifiedWhenReviewPublished.description: Label for the question that asks if the user wants to be notified when a PREreview is published following their request.

canContactWhenReviewPublished.message: We can email you whenever a PREreview is published for this preprint.
canContactWhenReviewPublished.description: Text shown alongside the question “Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?” that explains what will happen if the user selects “yes”.

canChangeContactWhenReviewPublished.message: You can change your preference at any time.
canChangeContactWhenReviewPublished.description: Text shown alongside the question “Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?” that explains that the user can change their preference.

beNotifiedWhenReviewPublishedMissing.message: Select yes if you would like to be notified
beNotifiedWhenReviewPublishedMissing.description: Error message shown when the user tries to submit the form “Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?” without selecting an option.

beNotifiedWhenReviewPublishedYes.description: Label for the positive option to the question “Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?”.

beNotifiedWhenReviewPublishedNo.description: Label for the negative option to the question “Would you like to be notified when a PREreview is published?”.

whatNameWouldYouLikeToUse.message: What name would you like to use?
whatNameWouldYouLikeToUse.description: Label for the question about the name the user would like to use.

backToPreprint.description: Label for the link that takes the user back to the preprint page.

selectNameYouWouldLikeToUse.message: Select the name that you would like to use
selectNameYouWouldLikeToUse.description: Error message shown when the user tries submit the “What name would you like to use?” form without selecting a name.

chooseBetweenOrcidNameAndPseudonym.message: You can choose between the name on your ORCID record or your PREreview pseudonym.
chooseBetweenOrcidNameAndPseudonym.description: Text shown alongside the question “What name would you like to use?” that explains the user can choose between their ORCID name and their PREreview pseudonym.

whatIsAPrereviewPseudonym.description: Heading for the section that explains what a PREreview pseudonym is.

prereviewPseudonymExplainer.message: A <definition>PREreview pseudonym</definition> is an alternate name you can use instead of your real name. It is unique and combines a random color and animal. Your pseudonym is ‘{pseudonym}.’
prereviewPseudonymExplainer.description: Text following the “What is a PREreview pseudonym?” heading explaining what a PREreview pseudonym is.

whyUseAPseudonym.message: Using your pseudonym, you can contribute to open preprint review without fearing retribution or judgment that may occur when using your real name. However, using a pseudonym retains an element of accountability.
whyUseAPseudonym.description: Text explaining why a user might want to use a PREreview pseudonym.

weWillLinkRequestToYourOrcid.message: We’ll link your request to your ORCID iD.
weWillLinkRequestToYourOrcid.description: Text that accompanies the public persona choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

weWillLinkRequestToOthersThatUseYourPseu.message: We’ll only link your request to others that also use your pseudonym.
weWillLinkRequestToOthersThatUseYourPseu.description: Text that accompanies the pseudonymous persona choice for the question “What name would you like to use?”.

requestPublished.description: Title of the page that confirms the user’s request for a PREreview has been published.

whatHappensNext.description: Heading for the section on the “Request published” page that explains what happens now the request is published.

whereYouCanSeeYourRequest.message: You’ll be able to see your request on our <listOfRequestsLink>list of requests</listOfRequestsLink> and <communitySlackLink>Community Slack</communitySlackLink> shortly.
whereYouCanSeeYourRequest.description: Text shown alongside the heading “What happens next” that explains where the user can see their request for a PREreview.

youCanRequestAPrereview.message: You can request a PREreview of {preprintTitle}. A PREreview is a review of a preprint and can vary from a few sentences to a lengthy report, similar to a journal-organized peer-review report.
youCanRequestAPrereview.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Request a PREreview” that explains what a PREreview is.

beforeYouStart.description: Heading for the section on the “Request a PREreview” page that explains what the user needs to know before they start their request.

weWillAskYouToLogInWithYourOrcid.message: We will ask you to log in with your ORCID iD. If you don’t have an iD, you can create one.
weWillAskYouToLogInWithYourOrcid.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Before you start”.

whatIsAnOrcid.description: Heading for the section that explains what an ORCID iD is.

orcidExplainer.message: An <orcidLinkAsDefinition>ORCID iD</orcidLinkAsDefinition> is a unique identifier that distinguishes you from everyone with the same or similar name.
orcidExplainer.description: Text following the “What is an ORCID iD?” heading explaining what an ORCID iD is.

asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.message: As you’ve already started, we’ll take you to the next step so you can carry on.
asYouHaveAlreadyStarted.description: Text shown alongside the heading “Before you start” that explains that the user has already started their request for a PREreview.
